vulnerability.title: Only Authorized Local User Accounts Exist on Operating System
vulnerability.description: Enterprise Application tends to use the server or virtual machine exclusively. Besides the default operating system user, there should be only authorized local users required by the installed software groups and applications that exist on the operating system. The authorized user list can be customized in the refine value variable var_accounts_authorized_local_users_regex. OVAL regular expression is used for the user list. Configure the system so all accounts on the system are assigned to an active system, application, or user account. Remove accounts that do not support approved system activities or that allow for a normal user to perform administrative-level actions. To remove unauthorized system accounts, use the following command: $ sudo userdel unauthorized_user
vulnerability.recommendation: Review and remediate: Only Authorized Local User Accounts Exist on Operating System

triage_decision.reason: Removing unauthorized local user accounts is a standard STIG hardening action that does not affect system boot, networking, or critical services, and follows established best practices for account management.
triage_decision.estimated_impact: authentication/authorization affected; Potential accidental removal of a legitimate account; Risk of lockout if the account is used for administrative tasks

review_feedback: Review output was not valid JSON. Raw: {"finding_id":"accounts_authorized_local_users","is_optimal":false,"approve":true,"feedback":"The remediation script will delete all non‑system users that are not matched by the authorized regex, ther...

previous_review_verdicts.feedback: The plan correctly identifies an unauthorized local user, backs up essential files, removes the user, and verifies its removal, which meets the SCAP rule requirement. However, the plan assumes system accounts are automatically authorized without explicitly checking the custom authorized list variable. For completeness, a check against var_accounts_authorized_local_users_regex would ensure that only truly authorized users remain.
concerns: Plan does not validate the user against the custom authorized list (var_accounts_authorized_local_users_regex).
Potential edge case where the user’s home directory or group may be needed by other services if not properly identified before deletion.
suggested_improvements: Add a step to parse and compare /etc/passwd against the authorized regex list before deciding to delete a user.
Use getent passwd to reliably list accounts rather than parsing /etc/passwd directly.
Log the deletion action and maintain an audit trail of removed accounts.
Confirm that the deleted user’s group is not shared with critical system processes before removing the group.
